title: GitLab Stock Fell on Major Layoffs. They Were Necessary to Enter the Agentic AI Era.
text: GitLab announced a sweeping organizational overhaul aimed at positioning the company for the emerging agentic AI era. CEO Bill Staples stated that bolting AI onto platforms not built for agents is the biggest mistake of this era. The restructuring includes reducing operational footprint across up to 30% of countries where GitLab has minimal presence, eliminating up to three management layers in certain departments to accelerate decision-making, reorganizing R&D into approximately 60 autonomous teams nearly double the current structure, and implementing AI agents to automate internal reviews and approvals. GitLab reported $955 million in fiscal 2026 revenue with 26% YoY growth and $220 million in free cash flow. The company maintains over $1.3 billion in cash reserves and initiated a $400 million share repurchase program. Despite stock declining 70% from all-time highs, analysts project the company could double within four years if valued at 20x forward free cash flow. The restructuring redirects savings toward AI infrastructure investments rather than reducing overall spending.
